title: Evangelion products and counterfeits
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion products and counterfeits             Quote Post by Darkman.exe213 » Thu Apr 07, 2011 1:05 am I know a good number of us just can't resist buying in on some of the crazy Eva merch out there. I myself have a growing collection that's starting to take up a whole shelf, comprised primarily of Asuka figures. But unfortunately, I've also seen a fair share of counterfeits and fakes, and it really is kinda demotivating to realize that your prized figure turned out to be counterfeit. :< So, I'd like to hear other people's experiences with counterfeit products, or how they learned to separate the counterfeits from the real things. Also, feel free to ask if you're not sure whether or not an Eva product you have it fake. Ways to discover a potential counterfeit product: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by SHINOBI-03 » Thu Apr 07, 2011 5:25 am This is a legitimate sticker, right? SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Thu Apr 07, 2011 7:07 am Hmmm I'm not really sure whether or not my new SKY HAWK FIGHTER figures are real or not... care to take a look? SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Kodaemon » Thu Apr 07, 2011 7:39 am I like how Asuka is apparently a crude and impetuous flying bird. Anyway, the only statue I have, a Gothic Lolita ver. of Asuka from Kotobukiya, doesn't have a sticker, but is certainly legit, as it was part of the 1.11 limited edition here.

[]:             Quote Post by Travosaga » Thu Apr 07, 2011 7:48 am I've had a bit of experience with figures and fakes before. One of the most common signs is in the paint. Fakes will often have overspray that gets into the eyes or underspray in the joints. Also you should look for miscolored pieces or pieces that are overly shiny which is something you see from cheaper paint. You should also look at the way it's assembled. Even 1/8 and 1/6 figures have a few pieces that come together often around the head and hair pieces. Are the joints sloppy or does it have a larger than normal gap? Those are also signs of a fake. Check also to see if it stands properly. All of the knockoffs I've had in my possession have some kind of standing problem where they always look like they are leaning because the legs are warped or the pegs in the stand won't fit properly into their holes because they weren't cut properly. Another thing I suggest when buying from eBay or something is to check where it's coming from. Never ever buy something coming out of china or Indonesia. Every thing I've ever gotten from there was a fake. What they will do is post a picture of a legit figure from the manufacturers website so it looks like the real deal but instead you get sent a cheap looking knockoff. I try to only buy from Japan or U.S.A. Also, check inside the box (if applicable) and look for the things that are normally inserted into them. This will include a Japanese booklet with some kind of instructions or warranty info and often times a small ad of sorts that shows off some other figures in that line or from a different series. A knockoff most likely wouldn't have those inserts. Finally, you should check the price. If it seems like it's too good to be true, then it probably is. You want something that is in the price range of others like it. When shopping at conventions, I highly recommend carrying a smart device that you can get on the Internet with. You can check prices and find similar product to compare it to. A smartphone is an invaluable tool for the modern convention shopper. Pay attention to the figures themselves because it isn't unheard of for somebody to use a box from a real figure and swap in the knock off. And yes shinobi, that sticker looks legit to me.

[]:             Quote Post by Darkman.exe213 » Thu Apr 07, 2011 1:27 pm toe mash wrote: Hmmm I'm not really sure whether or not my new SKY HAWK FIGHTER figures are real or not... care to take a look? SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Thu Apr 07, 2011 2:33 pm Darkman.exe213 wrote: I'd hate to say it, but those are counterfeit

[]:             Quote Post by Darkman.exe213 » Thu Apr 07, 2011 3:19 pm toe mash wrote: Jee, ya think? I thought the "SKY HAWK FIGHTER" title on the box gave it away, lol.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Thu Apr 07, 2011 3:36 pm On the "New Century Skyhawk Fighter" thing: The original chinese title of Evangelion in chinese(They even had a mandarin dub for eva, link for the OP, it´s terrible ) is 新世纪天鹰战士(Xin Shi Ji Tian Ying Zhan Shi), which can be translated into New Century Eagle/Hawk Warrior. They probably thought that Eva was another crazy Japanese cartoon where kids in robots fight stuff and left out the whole evangelion thing and changed the first few lines of the OP from "Cruel Angel" to "Beautiful Angel" and stuff.(Afaik, the last few episodes got heavy censoring and stuff, e.g.:graphic violence, Shinji running into AU!Rei without the whole "he peeked at my panties!" thing, etc.) Since the chinese are even worse in english than the Japanese, they probably gave it a super-literal english translation to make it sound cool. Therefore, the box says "New Century Sky Hawk Fighter". The new chinese name for Eva doesn´t make too much sense either, Kodansha Taiwan now calls it: 新世纪福音战士(Xin Shi Ji Fu Yin Zhan Shi) which literally means "New Century Evangelion Warrior", which can ALSO be alternatively interpreted into "New Century Evangelizing Warrior". Yeah. I just now completely forgot why I wrote all this. Edit:Oh yeah, I remember now: That infamous eva toy might not be a fake in the strictest sense after all.

[]:             Quote Post by shaneva002 » Thu Apr 07, 2011 3:47 pm Travosaga wrote: Never ever buy something coming out of china

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Thu Apr 07, 2011 4:19 pm InstrumentalityOne wrote: That infamous eva toy might not be a fake in the strictest sense after all.

[]:             Quote Post by MasterRah » Thu Apr 07, 2011 5:29 pm toe mash wrote: Hmmm I'm not really sure whether or not my new SKY HAWK FIGHTER figures are real or not... care to take a look? SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Thu Apr 07, 2011 9:05 pm InstrumentalityOne wrote: The new chinese name for Eva doesn´t make too much sense either, Kodansha Taiwan now calls it: 新世纪福音战士(Xin Shi Ji Fu Yin Zhan Shi) which literally means "New Century Evangelion Warrior", which can ALSO be alternatively interpreted into "New Century Evangelizing Warrior".

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 07, 2011 9:31 pm symbv wrote: And without the 战士 addition, the title would just look too much a religious treatise than a giant robot anime...

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 07, 2011 9:58 pm Darkman.exe213 : I have the Puchi Eva Unison Training Asuka (and Shinji) along with their boxes. I just double-checked the boxes/base you have the real deal. I ordered mine directly from a Japanese site (forget which one, but it's one I frequent) so I know it's legit.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Fri Apr 08, 2011 7:48 am symbv wrote: But 新世纪 is not too off.

[]:             Quote Post by visionfactory » Fri Apr 08, 2011 2:38 pm Indeed, the Japanese evangelion logo on Sky hawk fighter package says "ev u ngelion"

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Fri Apr 08, 2011 4:41 pm visionfactory wrote: Indeed, the Japanese evangelion logo on Sky hawk fighter package says "ev u ngelion"

[]:             Quote Post by Darkman.exe213 » Thu Apr 14, 2011 12:58 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: Darkman.exe213 : I have the Puchi Eva Unison Training Asuka (and Shinji) along with their boxes. I just double-checked the boxes/base you have the real deal. I ordered mine directly from a Japanese site (forget which one, but it's one I frequent) so I know it's legit.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Fri Dec 14, 2012 11:04 am I have several bootlegs that I know of: - large wallscroll (1296C bootleg) - I have found wallscrolls in general to be problematic since most of them are bootlegs and it's very easy to pick up fakes - a couple laminated posters (in Misc. at bottom) - easy to spot and the cheap prices are a giveway -a metallic pencil box , which actually doesn't look too bad. Even says who the Chinese manufacturer was. -a set of playing cards a Korean letter puzzle (in Misc. at bottom) - not sure about this one but it seems iffy As for figures, I have a couple of the Real Models that were done not by Sega, the original manufacturer, but by Animetrix with a Business Extension Corp. (BEC) logo on the front. They use different Gainax stickers than the original Real Models and a couple are even on Amazon . BEC seems to be legit and aside from the different stickers and manufacturer logos the figures are practically the same as the original Real Models.
